purpose: Turn a pasted GitHub URL into a guided first encounter with the two-volume ph-civ artifact.
reader_promise: In ten stops, the reader sees Volume I discover historical laws through civilizational memory and Volume II expose those laws under modern pressure.
continue_prompt: Continue the first_tour by opening civ-07 as the Homeric memory system: explain why it matters, how to read it, what not to overclaim, and where the reader can go next.

phases.title: Volume I Literary Spine
phases.function: Show how literary memory becomes civilizational grammar without claiming literature alone drives history.

phases.title: Close-Reading Reinforcement
phases.function: Return to Homer and Dante as reading engines so the spine is not only a survey path.

phases.title: Volume II Application Exposure
phases.function: Show the laws under geopolitical, incentive, Tolstoy-coda, and infrastructure pressure while keeping application routes bounded.

stops.reader_question: How does Homer turn heroic action into a civilizational memory system?
stops.next_action: Study civ-07 through its card, transcript, commentary, and future museum room.

stops.reader_question: What changes when Homeric memory becomes Roman public duty and imperial destiny?
stops.next_action: Follow the hinge from inherited epic to civilizational transmission.

stops.reader_question: How does Dante make civilization's inner order navigable?
stops.next_action: Trace sacred order as imaginative architecture.

stops.reader_question: How does Shakespeare join speech, legitimacy, theater, and imperial expansion?
stops.next_action: Read language as a power-bearing civilizational medium.

stops.reader_question: What does a closer Homer reading add that the civilizational survey cannot?
stops.next_action: Return from spine to close reading before moving further.

stops.reader_question: How does Dante become a machine for sorting desire, guilt, punishment, and ascent?
stops.next_action: Use close reading to strengthen the Dante node.

stops.reader_question: What happens when civilizational patterns meet chokepoints, proxies, platforms, and escalation?
stops.next_action: Enter Volume II application without treating the route as live operational analysis.

stops.reader_question: How do shipping, energy, deterrence, and narrative become pressure variables?
stops.next_action: Translate historical grammar into a bounded incentive field.

stops.reader_question: Why does Tolstoy enter as an Anna Karenina coda rather than a dedicated Tolstoy lecture?
stops.next_action: Preserve the Tolstoy caveat while completing the literary spine exposure.

stops.reader_question: How do finance, networks, nuclear power, and sacred geography become a contested system of meaning?
stops.next_action: Close the seed by seeing application pressure without overclaiming certainty.

closing_choices: open the ph-mus museum room for the current route
guardrails: Homer to Tolstoy is the Volume I literary spine, not a side corridor.
sh-16 is a Tolstoy endpoint via an Anna Karenina coda, not a dedicated Tolstoy lecture.
Application routes are public orientation only, not live operational analysis.
Every stop should return to the relevant card, transcript, and commentary before making chapter-specific claims.
